prep.summary: Note first that your folder contains no documents explicitly labeled "EA offsite meetings"—you must instead leverage recent technical leadership and Board of Directors notes to prepare for the standup. Prioritize immediate modernization demands—specifically the upcoming Project IVA employee pilot and the paused CMT/telematics forensic evaluations—while enforcing strict accountability for personal AI developer tools. Address the deep architectural ownership gaps—including the stalled resiliency pilots and the optimization sandbox funding constraints—to prevent downstream integration failures.
tasks: Establish a formal engagement process—utilizing documented meeting minutes—to hold business partners accountable for process requirements.
Partner with Michelle Carter to resolve the MII/residency team alignment issues before the Thursday deadline.
Deploy Grafana dashboards featuring 10 to 15 real-time metrics for the Pay Claim team within the next two weeks.
Formulate consumption-based chargebacks for high-end AI models to curb escalating infrastructure spend.
Implement automated batch data-validation jobs to flag incomplete change records before formal Change Review Group evaluations.
prep.title: Tech fellow standup

prep.summary: You must prepare for two major gaps in your recent records—neither a direct summary of a Deepak meeting nor notes on the Unsworth departure calibrations exist in your current folder. You need to align with Claire on the pending Resiliency level-set meeting with Deepak—originally flagged as a critical follow-up on May 29. Use this 1:1 to address the Unsworth departure directly—providing your perspective on team calibration and structural transitions during this leadership vacuum.
tasks: Schedule the pending business alignment meeting with Deepak to level-set on the stalled Resiliency and NART pilot processes.
Establish a consistent business engagement framework—such as formal meetings with recorded minutes—to hold partners accountable.
Address the calibration impact of the Unsworth departure directly with Claire to clarify team alignment.
Define the next steps for the telematics data framework transition to AWS and Iceberg architectures.

prep.summary: You must review your last check-in with Ray on May 29th—which highlights paused telematics projects, stalled resiliency pilots, and unplanned business dashboards causing architectural friction. None of the retrieved work notes explicitly mention Sai—meaning you must use this monthly sync to align him on these unresolved technical bottlenecks. You need to establish a formal business engagement process—and define a steady-state framework for the 2025 telematics data transition.
tasks: Establish a consistent business engagement process—including formal meetings with recorded minutes—to involve architecture earlier and avoid project delays.
Define the transition strategy and steady-state framework—specifically trickle versus bulk loading—for the 2025 Telematics data migration to AWS Iceberg.
Align with Sai on the stalled Resiliency and NART pilots—determining whether your team must drive the PO3 process mapping directly.
Propose consumption-based chargebacks for AI and LLM governance—curbing the unnecessary use of expensive, high-end models across business units.

prep.summary: You must address the stalled Resiliency/NART pilots—no direct notes from a past meeting with Deepak exist in your files, but the May 29 update mandates scheduling an alignment session with him immediately. Your broader operational landscape remains highly fragmented—unplanned projects like TiVo are causing friction, while P&C sandbox discussions highlight severe environment parity constraints. You also need to transition telematics data to a steady-state framework—trickle versus bulk—while establishing governance for generative AI use cases.
tasks: Schedule the alignment meeting with Deepak—the key business stakeholder—to resolve the stalled Resiliency/NART PO3 process mapping.
Establish a formalized engagement process—complete with meeting minutes—to hold business partners accountable for unplanned pilot bypasses.
Define the steady-state data framework for migrating the 2025 telematics data to the new AWS, Iceberg, and Domino Nexus architectures.
Implement consumption-based chargebacks for high-end LLM usage to prevent runaway costs across teams.
Prune the ServiceNow POC presentation deck—specifically hiding slides 6, 7, 15, and 515—prior to the review with Dan Griffiths.

focus_html:
Focus · Next up
8:00 AM — 9:00 AM

Tech fellow standup

Prep

Note first that your folder contains no documents explicitly labeled "EA offsite meetings"—you must instead leverage recent technical leadership and Board of Directors notes to prepare for the standup. Prioritize immediate modernization demands—specifically the upcoming Project IVA employee pilot and the paused CMT/telematics forensic evaluations—while enforcing strict accountability for personal AI developer tools. Address the deep architectural ownership gaps—including the stalled resiliency pilots and the optimization sandbox funding constraints—to prevent downstream integration failures.

  • Establish a formal engagement process—utilizing documented meeting minutes—to hold business partners accountable for process requirements.
  • Partner with Michelle Carter to resolve the MII/residency team alignment issues before the Thursday deadline.
  • Deploy Grafana dashboards featuring 10 to 15 real-time metrics for the Pay Claim team within the next two weeks.
  • Formulate consumption-based chargebacks for high-end AI models to curb escalating infrastructure spend.
  • Implement automated batch data-validation jobs to flag incomplete change records before formal Change Review Group evaluations.
